DISCOUNTS BOOST BRAND LOYALTY
articles.summary: Price discounts, loyalty points, and experiential rewards all lift customer loyalty. Discounts generate the strongest boost, and loyalty points and experiences outperform BOGO offers. Tactics that build emotional engagement create longer-lasting connections than incentives focused only on savings. Still, heavy discounting can weaken brand perception, train customers to wait for deals, and undermine long-term value. Promotions work best when used with intention rather than as routine price cuts.

articles.title: TRUST AND PERSONALIZATION KEY TO WINNING OVER B2B BUYERS
articles.summary: B2B buyers now rely more on personal factors like trust and values, which have risen in importance by 10% since 2021. Professional drivers still influence 58% of decisions, but personal and societal drivers account for 42%, and many buyers start with a preset list where 92% end up purchasing from someone already on it. Most marketers think they deliver strong brand communication, yet only 39% of buyers feel that suppliers tailor their offer to their needs, and only 5% of marketers achieve true hyperpersonalization. Brands also struggle with onboarding, which reinforces the growing importance of trust and personalized experiences in winning deals.

articles.title: THE TEXT OVERLAY FORMULA TO OPTIMIZE ADS
articles.summary: Optimized text overlays on videos and GIFs can increase clicks by up to 127%, and the best results come from placing medium sized text near the edges rather than the center. The research finds that X posts perform best when text covers 24% of the frame, Facebook sees up to 27% higher CTRs with medium text, and Instagram engagement peaks when text covers 11% of the frame. Static images work differently and tend to perform best when larger text is centered. These effects come from how viewers process the balance between text and imagery, which increases attention and engagement.

articles.title: WHAT'S IN A NAME? A LOT, ACTUALLY. HERE'S HOW TO PICK THE RIGHT ONE FOR YOUR COMPANY
articles.summary: A strong brand name helps a company stand out, but the process requires time, structure, and clear criteria rather than a single burst of inspiration. Founders should treat naming as a month-long effort that starts with solid positioning, a detailed naming brief, and a broad brainstorm that produces hundreds of options. A shortlist should then be evaluated for trademark risk, domain availability, distinctiveness, pronunciation, and longevity. The final step is legal clearance, which often includes a knockout search and a comprehensive search before deciding whether to file a trademark.

INTRODUCING GEMINI 3
articles.summary: Google just launched Gemini 3, its most intelligent model to date. For content teams, this means richer, more personalized experiences in Search, while developers gain a more autonomous agent-building stack through Google Antigravity. Gemini 3, focused on deeper reasoning, stronger multimodal capabilities, and long-horizon planning, powers dynamic UI generation, real-time simulations, and zero-shot coding.

articles.title: MOBBIN
articles.summary: Mobbin is a design inspiration platform with a large library of more than 500K screens and 1K apps. It helps users quickly find real product patterns, explore complete user flows with animations, and study transitions and micro-interactions. Users can walk through flows, copy designs directly into Figma, save collections, and take notes. The platform, used by major design teams, serves as a fast way to research best practices and stay current on UI and UX standards.

articles.title: IT'S TIME TO RETHINK WHAT THE FEED IS FOR
articles.summary: Under Old Social, reach was linked to follower size. Now, unless a post appeals to non-followers, it rarely gains traction. Brands must adapt to a recommendation-based environment where consistency alone no longer works. Some have embraced a high-volume approach, flooding feeds with clips or creator-led content to increase their chances of being picked up. Others focus on episodic storytelling to build momentum and retain viewer interest, much like programming a TV show. Large brands like Netflix and Amazon are leading this shift by developing recurring series, while smaller brands like Little Joy or Craighill are seeing real sales lift from serialized posts.

articles.title: WHATSAPP OWNS INDIA
articles.summary: WhatsApp has become essential business infrastructure in India despite being built for consumers. It forces small businesses to rely on manual workarounds, hacks, and risky mod apps because true automation sits behind the restrictive and costly Business API. Meta's shifting policies, delivery caps, and pricing make the platform unstable, and its ad-driven incentives prevent features that would separate personal and professional use. An ONDC-style messaging protocol that enables interoperability across apps would enable businesses to use purpose-built tools without abandoning WhatsApp, reducing dependence on a single platform.

articles.title: GOOGLE CEO WARNS THAT NO TECH GIANT IS SAFE IF AN AI BUBBLE BURSTS
articles.summary: An AI bust would affect every major tech firm, including Google. Today's investment surge shows both rational optimism and signs of irrational exuberance similar to the dotcom era. Analysts and Wall Street leaders predict a correction that could impact markets more broadly. Investors are overexcited even as AI remains a profound long-term shift. Current AI tools like Gemini can still make errors despite Alphabet's recent valuation surge.

articles.summary: Make webpage content scannable, readable, and logical to optimize it for LLMs and humans.
